summary: Educational grading/reporting app with multi-term percentage calculation, 9-point grading system, subject analysis, and certificate generation features.
architectural_notes: Combined percentage logic: sum adjusted term percentages per grade divided by term count (2 or 3)
Certificate percentage formula: (original_percentage + 94) / 2 per term per grade

existing_features.title: Update the Combined Percentage view to compute an overall percentage per grade by averaging the adjusted percentages across all available terms: divide the sum of adjusted term percentages by 2 when only two terms exist, or by 3 when a third term is present. Display the result as a single 'Overall %' column/row per grade.

Update the Nine Scale Grades view to display a grade (1–9) for each subject, for each term, for each grade level. Apply the grading scale: 90–100 → 9, 80–89 → 8, 70–79 → 7, 60–69 → 6, 50–59 → 5, 40–49 → 4, 30–39 → 3, 20–29 → 2, 0–19 → 1. Grades must be shown per subject per term per grade, not aggregated.

Enhance the Subject Analysis view to show, for each subject across all grades: (1) average raw marks (e.g. 73), (2) average percentage, (3) highest raw marks and corresponding percentage, (4) lowest raw marks and corresponding percentage. Add filter controls for grade and term so the user can narrow results to a specific grade and/or term.

Add a Certificate section (or update the existing CertificateView) that generates a certificate for each grade and term. The certificate percentage is calculated as: (original term percentage + 94) / 2. Display the certificate with that computed percentage. Provide grade and term filter controls so the user can view certificates for a specific grade and term.

new_feature_requests.summary: Combined percentage: combine all adjusted percentages per grade and divide by number of terms (2 for 2 terms, 3 if third term mentioned) to get overall percentage per grade
Nine grading system: assign grade (1-9) per subject per term per grade where 90-100=9, 80-89=8, 70-79=7, etc.
Subject analysis: show average raw marks (e.g. 73), average percentage, highest and lowest raw marks, and highest/lowest percentage for each subject across all grades, filterable by grade and term
Certificate generation per term: certificate percentage = (original percentage + 94) / 2, filterable by grade and term
